hero_subtitle: SFTY links wallet activity, transaction risk, and sanctions alerts into a unified audit trail that supports compliance teams with defensible evidence and case workflows.

problem_title: Audit trails weaken across sanctions exposure
problem_description: Compliance teams struggle to preserve comprehensive evidence linking sanctions exposure alerts to transaction monitoring decisions. This fragmentation slows reviews and makes it harder to defend sanctions-related compliance outcomes during audits.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY connects sanctions alerts, wallet and transaction risk data, case decisions and reviewer actions into one traceable workflow that supports compliance teams managing sanctions exposure audits.
workflow_title: From wallet risk to sanctions evidence
workflow_description: SFTY moves wallet risk and transaction alerts into structured cases with documented evidence to support sanctions exposure decisions and compliance reporting.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects sanctions exposure signals with transactions, cases and decisions to preserve audit evidence and provide a clear compliance operating layer for VASPs.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASP compliance teams by preserving audit evidence and orchestrating provider outputs within controlled, enterprise-grade workflows for sanctions exposure decisions.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for VASP compliance teams?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides VASPs a compliance control layer that connects KYT, sanctions screening, transaction risk and case management into connected, auditable workflows for sanctions exposure monitoring.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY handle sanctions exposure workflows?
faq_2_answer: SFTY aggregates wallet and transaction risk signals, creates linked cases for sanctions alerts, supports review routing, decision logging, and maintains evidence for compliance workflows.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves decision logs, case history and provider output snapshots to support audit-ready evidence supporting sanctions exposure reviews and regulatory inspections.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing compliance tools?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY works above existing KYT, screening, KYC and Travel Rule providers, connecting their outputs into a coherent operational and compliance workflow stack.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace sanctions screening providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace sanctions providers; it connects and structures their outputs into workflows, cases and reports within one compliance operating environment.
final_title: Preserve sanctions exposure evidence with SFTY
final_description: Review how SFTY connects wallet risk, transaction monitoring and sanctions alerts into audit-ready cases and evidence workflows.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
